title: Summary of updates to T&Cs and Quality Charter 2025
bodyHtml:
Starting 4 August 2025, annual T&Cs and Quality Charter updates will be enforced. 
You will find below the major changes compared to the last update (February 2025 for T&Cs and August 2024 for the Charter).
 

T&Cs updates: 

  • Design: We’ve redesigned our Sellers Terms and Conditions to make them more attractive and easier to read. It’s the same design as the Quality Charter.
  • Monthly subscription fees: Increase from 50 to 75€/£/$.
    • To allow BM to invest in enhanced seller support services, better marketplace infrastructure.
  • Deletion of Back Repair in the EU, and of Back Ship in the US
    • This update reflects the deletion of these services that has been in effect for several months. We're focusing resources on core marketplace features that directly impact seller success.
  • Launch Phase: Specification that it can be ended with a 1-day notice.
    • Focus on quality so that only high quality Sellers are onboarded
  • Trade-in Logistics fees (EU only): updates and increase of the logistics fees (+1€ or +2€), depending on the country of delivery of the product, i.e. the location where the Seller has their warehouse.
    • To reflect current market conditions and operational costs in different regions.
    • If the Seller is based in FR/DE/ES: Smartphones & Audio 11.90€ / Tablets 13.90€ / MacBooks & Consoles 14.90€
    • If the Seller is based in any other EU country: Smartphones & Audio 12.90€ / Tablets 13.90€ / MacBooks & Consoles 15.90€
  • Trade-in updates to BackBox: it now takes into account (i) the price offer, (ii) the Refurbisher’s counteroffer rate, and (iii) the Refurbisher’s circularity rate
    • Rewards to Sellers who maintain high circularity rates and competitive pricing, creating fairer competition for winning the BackBox position. 
  • Updates to the trade-in invoicing and payment terms article to account for trade-in
    • Clearer payment processes for trade-in transactions 
  • Updates to the deferred payout to take into consideration Trade-in
    • Better integration of trade-in payments with deferred payout structures
  • Back Market Personal Data Charter: Sellers can now find best practices for protecting Buyers' personal data in the Personal Data Charter.
    • Clear data protection guidelines help sellers maintain compliance with privacy regulations.

 

Quality Charter Sales part updates: 

General update: From now on, we will be counting only business days.

Article 1.1 Item quality control

  • Smartphones batteries in the US & UK must have initial capacities of at least 80% and no more than 700 cycle counts. Tablet, smart watches, and laptop batteries must have initial capacities of at least 85% and no more than 650 cycle counts.
  • MacBooks and iMacs must be equipped with MacOs 11 or higher
  • All Windows PC computers (laptops and desktops) must be reset with a Windows  11  license (a valid Windows 11  item key must have been provided or an OEM key validated during installation)
  • All iPhones and iPads must be restored to the latest supported iOS version released not more than 2 months prior to your customers’ purchases.

 

Article 1.3 Appearance: cleaning and assessing
Engravings are not permitted on products graded Premium, Excellent (Mint & Very Good), or Good. For products in Fair grade (Fair & Stallone), engravings are allowed provided the text does not contain personal data, discriminatory content, hate speech, obscene material, or any other inappropriate messages. Such engraving does not void the customers’ right of withdrawal.

 

Article 1.7 Accessories
For smartphones, tablets, and iPads: a compatible charging cable with surge protection is required.

  • We recommend using a cable of standard length, not exceeding 1 m (40 in), for quality reasons.
  • For ecological reasons, headphones and wall plugs are not mandatory when selling smartphones, tablets, and iPads.

 

Article 2.1 Shipping options
New table with preparation and transit times for all the categories:

  • Category S items must be shipped the same day if the order is placed before cut-off time
  • The default cut-off time is 1:00 PM. You may update this setting in your Back-Office to a later cut-off time if it better reflects your actual operations. For more information, consult the Seller Support Center.
  • We recommend using a secure delivery method (such as signature, PIN code, or QR code) for all orders over 150 €/£/$. Incidents during delivery are significantly reduced when these services are used.
  • We recommend you include the Back market packing slip

 

Previous Article 2.2 “Order preparation and shipment” was deleted, all the information is now available in article 2.1

 

Article 2.2 Serial number and IMEI code

  • You must provide IMEIs for smartphones and the serial numbers for iPads, tablettes, MacBooks and Laptops
  • These numbers must be added in the receipts as well

 

Article 2.3 Sending the receipt

  • The receipts must be issued in the customer’s language. If you fail to provide the receipt in the customer language the customer will be entitled to the goodwill gesture as if you have not provided the receipt
  • If you fail to provide a receipt containing the required information or proof of refund within 2 business days following Back Market’s reminder, the customer is entitled to a free cancellation of their order or a goodwill gesture valued at up to €50/$50/£50

 

Article 3.3 Delivery issues
In cases of shipping-related disputes on forward shipment, it is your sole responsibility to file a claim with your shipping provider and to provide all required documentation (unless you are using our BackShip service).
Back Market will:

  • Allow customers up to 14 days to report cases of damage, empty packages, or incorrect/missing products in specific cases (customer vacation, absent, etc).
  • Consider a package lost after 6 days without tracking updates.
  • No longer provide or require a certificate of non-receipt from customers.

The agent can ask your customer for a police report only when there is a doubt on delivery fraud and if you provide the delivery confirmation within 2 business days following the agent's request for a Proof of delivery.

Upon notification from the agent following case assessment, you must initiate either a refund or shipment of a replacement product within 1 business day, based on the customer's selection. This obligation applies irrespective of the status, progress, or outcome of any shipping provider investigation.

If your shipping provider rejects your claim due to failure to provide required documents, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, subject to the following conditions:

  • Submit your compensation request within thirty (30) days of the order refund date.
  • Provide all information and documentation reasonably requested by Back Market agents.
  • Submit proof that your claim was rejected by the shipping provider or insurer specifically due to missing documents that Back Market was responsible for providing.
  • Have used a delivery method requiring signature, PIN code, or QR code verification.

If you provide the proof that you held ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel, compensation is capped to the amount specified in the insurance policy. 
If you are not able to provide proof of ad valorem insurance, compensation will be limited to 23€/$/GBP per Kg (e.g: If you shipped a 2Kg parcel, then you’ll get back 46€). 

 

Article 3.4.2 Seller contractual warranty: solutions offered
If your customer requests a repair or replacement, you have up to 3 business days from the receipt of the item to repair or replace it and upload the tracking number in the Back Office. The item must be shipped back to the customer no later than the end of the 4th business day—the sooner, the better. For laptops and home appliances, you have up to 5 business days to complete the repair or replacement and upload the tracking number, and the item must be shipped the next business day at the latest. You can provide a refund if a repair or replacement isn’t an option.

In the EU, if a customer submits a technical service request within 6 months of receiving their product and the battery capacity is below 80%, you must replace the battery in addition to performing any other necessary repairs. If you are unable to complete the repair promptly, you must offer a replacement device or a refund.

In the US and UK, the same obligation applies, but the period during which the customer can submit a technical service request is limited to 3 months from the date of receipt.


Article 3.4.3 Multiple-defect policy
If your multiple defects rate is deemed too high by Back Market, we reserve the right to deactivate the repair and/or replacement options for your account. For more detailed information, please refer to the Seller Support Center

 

Article 3.4.4 Seller contractual warranty exclusions
The seller contractual warranty doesn’t cover the following cases:

  • When the product is oxidized or broken
  • When 1 or more of the components of the product have been manipulated by the customer or a third party not authorized by you or Back Market 

However, products with swollen batteries cannot be shipped due to the risk of explosion. Therefore, if a swollen battery is suspected, our team will diagnose the issue and instruct your customer to visit a local repair shop. The repair shop will follow provided guidelines, and costs will be pre-approved according to market standards, not exceeding the value of the device.

If the device is repairable, you are responsible for covering the invoice from the repair shop, regardless of whether the battery is ultimately found to be swollen. If the device cannot be repaired, it will be returned to you with the battery removed, at your expense, and the customer will receive a full refund. In the event that a certified OEM workshop determines the device poses a safety hazard and that battery removal is too dangerous, the customer will be refunded in full and the device will not be returned.


Article 3.5.2 Reception of the product and applicable deadlines
If your customer requests a repair or replacement, you have up to 3 business days from the receipt of the item to repair or replace it and upload the tracking number in the Back Office. The item must be shipped back to the customer no later than the end of the 4th business day—the sooner, the better. For laptops and home appliances, you have up to 5 business days to complete the repair or replacement and upload the tracking number, and the item must be shipped the next business day at the latest.

If you repeatedly fail to meet the repair or replacement deadlines—after receiving at least two warnings from Back Market—we may deactivate the repair option for your account. For more detailed information, please refer to the Seller Support Center.

For parcels that look damaged:

  • Do not refuse the parcel. Instead, express reservations on the proof of delivery when you receive the parcel.
  • Take photos of the outside of the parcel, including a photo with the label visible. Open the parcel and take photos of the content (video works as well).
  • Go to the AfterSales conversation related to the order, and use the preset buttons to declare a problem. Choose "Damaged package and product in transit" and include the photos.
  • The agent will take over the case. If the device is refunded to the customer, you will receive a compensation corresponding to the repair cost of the damage, as stated in the SSC.

 

Article 3.5.5 Order refunds
In cases where accessories are missing (not returned by the customer), you must issue a full refund and submit a refund request through the Seller Support Center.
You can’t deduct the following costs from the refund:

  • Restocking fees
  • Return costs in case your customer refuses the package
  • Missing accessories

 

Article 3.5.7 Declaring a warranty exclusion
If you believe a warranty exclusion applies, you must:

  • Report the warranty exclusion on the Customer Care after-sales platform via the action buttons within 2 business days in case the customer requests a refund and within 3 business days in case the customer requests repair or replacement for all categories. (Reporting a warranty exclusion by message will not work. You must report the exclusion via the action buttons.)
  • Document and explain the warranty exclusion within the same deadline for it to be valid.
  • Return the product to your customer only after the confirmation of Warranty exclusion by Back Market and within 2 business days after this confirmation. Paid repairs are forbidden in this case.

You must use the preset buttons to report any issues with returns by the deadline or the order can be automatically refunded. Any declaration done without using the preset buttons will not be accepted.

If your customer disputes the warranty exclusion, or if Back Market rejects the exclusion due to doubts about its legitimacy, you will be required to proceed with the repair or replacement within the next 2 business days, or issue an immediate refund, as if no warranty exclusion had been applied.

If there is any doubt regarding the authenticity of the device and no IMEI (or serial number) has been provided in the Back Office, Back Market will side with the customer.

For more information refer to the article “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” in the Seller Support center.

 

Article 3.6 Goodwill gestures
To increase the probability that the customer will keep the device, for all partial refunds or goodwill gestures, the amount will be rounded up to the nearest 5€ or $5. For example, a refund of 7€ will be rounded up to 10€. This does not apply to partial refunds for late express shipping, which will be refunded at the exact shipping cost. 

 

Article 3.6.2 In case of missing or defective accessories
If an accessory is not listed in Appendix 2, and its value is less than 40 EUR/GBP/USD and no more than 10% of the item's price, the Agent may offer a commercial gesture based on the current market price, without needing to request the exact amount from the seller.

 

Article 3.6.3 In case of appearance grade discrepancy

 

Grade ordered

Premium

Excellent

Good

Grade received by the customer

Excellent

Good

10% of the product sale price

5% of the product sale price

Fair

15% of the product sale price

10% of the product sale price

5% of the product sale price

 

The minimum commercial gesture is 5 EUR/GBP.
In case of the dirty item or item worse than fair, the Agent may propose 5% commercial gesture.


Quality Charter Trade-in updates

Article 1.4: Trade-in grades
Engraved MacBook or side with stickers are considered as cracked (grade 3 or grade 5, depending on functional state).

 

Article 2.2.2: Packages received empty or non-received

  • In case of packages marked as delivered but not received, or received empty, you now need to provide directly in the chat and right away the Certificate of non-receipt.
  • For damaged package received, if you have a listing for the product in the received grade, you must do a counteroffer and the counteroffer price must, at least, either match the listing price for that grade or be 80% of the current Backbox price.

 

Article 2.2.3: Cleaning and performing checks
In the event that you receive the wrong model with higher market value than expected or multiple devices:

  • Always inform the Back Market customer by message (this includes indicating to the Back Market customer the model of the product actually received)
  • Make a counteroffer for the wrong model either in line with your listing prices if you list this model, or with the product's market value. 

 

Article 2.4.1 Deadline for processing trade-in
If you’re unable to process the Trade-in order by the deadline, once the Back Office clearly warns you about not meeting processing deadlines and you are late on processing an order, Back Market will proceed with the payment of the initial offer price on your behalf. You must track the orders at risk in your Back Office.

 

Article 2.4.2 Conditions of Trade-in

  • If you’re unable to carry out any action to process the order (validation, customer action required, unprocessable item, transport issue), you must inform Back Market without delay and justify your reasoning. If you don’t notify Back Market, the order may be considered as not yet processed and payment may be triggered.
  • Specific rules for products bought in grade 4 & 5 (see grade table in 1.4) :
    • If IMEI can't be found: you must pay the initial offer price
    • You can put on hold the order (for iCloud locked) or refuse a product (for blocklisted/rooted/counterfeit/greylisted) only if you are able to prove the issue to the customer (with IMEI check report or alternatives).  

 

Article 2.5.3 Making a counteroffer
You can now access the list of accepted testing softwares in our Seller Support Center HERE to help you enhance the accuracy and quality of your counteroffers.
There is one unique list of accepted softwares for both your Sales and your Trade-ins.

 

Article 2.8 Recovered product return
If you’re unable to return products by the deadline (1 business day), Back Market reserves the right to pay the initial offer price  on your behalf.
